patch.value: Merge-driver fence completeness: relationships/*.jsonl event stores uncovered and schema-added custom type folders silently drop driver coverage

buildMergeAttributePatterns covers relationship event store JSONL paths (default relationships/events.jsonl and custom relativePath stores) with an append-union driver; adding a custom item type via pm schema refreshes or flags the fence (auto-refresh where merge install already ran, otherwise actionable hint); validate (or the storage-integrity check) warns when committed .gitattributes fence coverage drifts from the active schema type folders + known JSONL stores; MERGE_SAFETY.md documents the coverage contract

Two coverage gaps in the PR#614 merge-driver fence (src/sdk/merge/install.ts buildMergeAttributePatterns):

1. Relationship event stores are never covered. The fence enumerates per-type item folders (*.toon/*.md), history/*.jsonl, settings.json, schema/*.json — but PR#587's RelationshipEventStore writes append-only JSONL at relationships/events.jsonl (default) or a caller-chosen relativePath. These files are tracked by default (not in the PM_GITIGNORE_BLOCK) and carry the same concurrent-append conflict class the pm-wc1r history driver fixed. Latent today (this workspace has no relationships/ dir yet) but any two branches appending relationship events will hard-conflict or, worse, resolve by default text merge and corrupt event ordering.

2. Type-folder enumeration is install-time-frozen. `pm schema` custom item types added AFTER `pm merge install` write items into folders with no merge attribute — silent fallback to git default merge, reintroducing exactly the pm-9q2t count-header corruption for those items. MERGE_SAFETY.md's "re-run pm merge install after adding custom types" is manual-only mitigation with no drift detection.

Fix directions (one class fix, not per-artifact patches): extend the pattern builder; refresh the fence from schema mutations where an install marker exists; teach validate/storage-integrity to diff committed fence coverage against active schema folders + known JSONL stores.

author: maintainer-agent
message: Multi-agent conflict-safety wave: implementing in one combined PR (pibw id entropy + i4fx fence completeness + iwsj repo adoption + x29o typed SDK inputs)

merge artifact help derived from the shared enum

value.note: fence coverage contract + relationship driver semantics

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Evidence: fence coverage contract now includes relationships/**/*.jsonl under new pm-relationship driver (eventId union + consecutive sequence renumbering so the strict store loader accepts merged streams); pm schema add-type/remove-type auto-refresh the installed fence (merge_fence_refreshed) or emit merge_fence_not_installed hint; validate storage_integrity audits committed fence vs active schema folders and warns validate_merge_fence_drift with exact missing/stale lines (warn-only when data is intact). Manual sandbox: driver renumbered 1,2 + 1,2 -> 1,2,3; schema add-type Spike refreshed fence; drift detected after out-of-band edit and cleared by pm merge install. Specs: merge-safety (16), schema-command (67), validate-command (91) green.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Strict-sequence JSONL stores (relationship events validate sequence === version+1 at load) can never be text-union-merged: any merge driver for them must renumber the merged stream consecutively, not just union lines. The pm-history driver was NOT reusable here — history chains re-anchor hashes, relationship streams renumber sequences; different invariants, different drivers.

value.author: codex-wide-sdk-20260719
value.text: Final merge-fence implementation: the installed fence assigns pm-relationship to every tracker JSONL path via .agents/pm/**/*.jsonl, then applies the more-specific history/*.jsonl pm-history override. This protects custom RelationshipEventStore relative paths and future schema folders without weakening hash-chain history handling. Focused tests cover install/repair/audit, arbitrary custom paths, relationship union/renumbering, timestamp ties, missing optional timestamps, malformed streams, and the real driver artifact. Full coverage is 100/100/100/100.

value.author: codex-wide-sdk-20260719
value.text: A relationship event store is customizable below the tracker root, so merge safety must fence the artifact class by general JSONL coverage with a specific history override, not by assuming a fixed relationships directory. Git attributes last-match specificity preserves the distinct history driver.

value.author: codex-wide-sdk-20260719
value.text: Greptile pre-push review 2b371c5d-ceb0-4948-8d0a-1b403891b2c5 identified two merge edge cases. Accepted: automatic fence refresh resolves the repository from the absolute pmRoot, proven while process.cwd() is a different git checkout; relationship timestamp ties now keep ours-before-theirs while stable sorting preserves each side's original append order. The pmRoot-derived git root made the prior outside-root branch impossible, so it was removed rather than artificially covered. Focused 25-test regression set and full 5,976-test 100/100/100/100 gate pass.

patch.value: Implemented complete JSONL merge fencing, relationship-event three-way union/renumbering, schema-triggered fence refresh, and drift auditing anchored to the tracker repository.
Every current or custom tracker relationship stream receives field-aware merge protection and schema changes cannot silently leave the committed fence stale.

All tracker JSONL selects pm-relationship with history-specific override; divergent streams preserve both sides, stable append ordering, and consecutive sequences; pmRoot-based refresh and drift tests pass.
Implemented and verified universal tracker JSONL fencing, relationship stream reconciliation, and tracker-root-anchored automatic refresh; Greptile clean and full coverage passed.
